meaning: n.前进,提升,预付款v.前进,提前,预付adj.前面的,预先的 预付(款项)
examples: We will advance you the money.
A 10% deposit is payable in advance.
The general gave the order to advance.
Advance tickets are cheaper but there is no solid data on the cost or number sold.
Plan at least six months in advance to track down ancestors and new relatives.
